meta.description: Private reference data for APAC AI Intelligence site

dataPoints.claim: Anthropic cumulative collected revenue ~$5B (all-time through 2024/early 2025)
dataPoints.notes: Sworn court affidavit — highest confidence. This is cumulative collected revenue, not ARR. Used to calibrate Anthropic's 2025 collected revenue estimate.

dataPoints.claim: Anthropic 2025 annualised revenue run rate ~$4.5B
dataPoints.notes: The Information reporting. Cross-checked against sworn affidavit: if cumulative ever is $5B, then 2025 collected could be $3.5-4.5B depending on ramp. We used $4.5B as the Sankey provider inflow from customers.

dataPoints.claim: OpenAI H1 2024 revenue ~$2.27B (derived from Microsoft revenue share)
dataPoints.notes: Zitron obtained Microsoft internal docs showing $454.7M H1 2024 revenue share from OpenAI. At 20% rev share → ~$2.27B OpenAI H1 revenue. Annualised with growth → $6B 2025 collected estimate.

dataPoints.claim: OpenAI 2025 collected revenue estimate ~$6B
dataPoints.notes: Derived from Zitron's H1 2024 data ($2.27B H1) + reported growth trajectory. NOT the $12.7B exit ARR figure widely reported. Actual collected likely $5-7B range. $6B is mid-estimate. Total OpenAI inflow on Sankey is $12B ($6B customer + $6B VC).

dataPoints.claim: OpenAI operating loss ~$6B in 2025
dataPoints.notes: Multiple reports indicate OpenAI losing ~$5-6B annually. Used $6B as VC subsidy in Sankey (VC/Investors → OpenAI flow).

dataPoints.claim: Anthropic operating loss ~$5.2B in 2025
dataPoints.notes: Anthropic total system cost estimated at $9.7B (inference + people/SG&A). Customer revenue $4.5B. Gap = $5.2B funded by VC rounds (Amazon, Google, etc). Sankey: VC → Anthropic = $5.2B.

dataPoints.claim: Total AI customer revenue (global, 2025) ~$15.6B
dataPoints.notes: Sum of all customer flows: Consumer $4.5B + SME $3B + Enterprise $8.1B = $15.6B. This is actual collected revenue for inference services, NOT ARR and NOT including training capex.

dataPoints.claim: VC operating subsidy to AI providers ~$12B in 2025
dataPoints.notes: The gap between customer revenue ($15.6B) and total operating costs ($27.6B). Breakdown: OpenAI $6B + Anthropic $5.2B + IaaS $0.3B + Google $0.5B. This is purely the operating gap, NOT training capex or GPU procurement.

dataPoints.claim: Inference compute costs ~$13B (47% of total AI system spend)
dataPoints.notes: Sum of all provider → Inference flows: OpenAI $7.2B (60%) + Anthropic $4.2B (43%) + IaaS $0.4B (50%) + Google $1.2B (48%) = $13B. NVIDIA captures majority of this via GPU pricing.

dataPoints.claim: Traditional SaaS AI features revenue ~$2.8B (2025)
dataPoints.sourceAuthor: APAC AI Intel (derived from deep analysis)
dataPoints.notes: Revenue from AI SKUs sold by traditional SaaS vendors: Microsoft M365 Copilot, Salesforce Einstein GPT, ServiceNow Now Assist, etc. Previously validated through deep analysis. Zitron is skeptical but doesn't have new data points beyond what we already calculated. Kept at $2.8B.

dataPoints.claim: Microsoft M365 Copilot H1 2024 revenue share to OpenAI: $454.7M
dataPoints.notes: From Microsoft internal documents obtained by Ed Zitron. This is the revenue share paid BY Microsoft TO OpenAI for inference, not Microsoft's total Copilot revenue. At ~20% rev share implies ~$2.27B OpenAI revenue from Microsoft channel alone in H1 2024.

dataPoints.claim: Hyperscaler cloud margin on AI model serving ~20%
dataPoints.notes: AWS Bedrock, Azure OpenAI, Google Vertex take ~20% margin on pass-through model serving. They use their own infrastructure (circular economics) and pay model providers for inference. Exact margin not publicly disclosed. $0.4B of $2B Cloud Channel flows to Margin.

dataPoints.claim: IaaS/open model provider gross margin 15-35%
dataPoints.notes: IaaS providers (Together AI, Fireworks, OpenRouter) have a thin positive gross margin on inference — better than closed frontier models which are negative. But zero operating margin — GM is entirely consumed by people and ops. Range based on infrastructure business comparables. Insufficient public data for higher confidence.

dataPoints.claim: Open models trail frontier by ~3 months
dataPoints.notes: Llama, DeepSeek, Qwen releases consistently follow frontier model capabilities by approximately 3 months. This gap has been narrowing over time.

dataPoints.claim: Llama downloads exceed 1.2B
dataPoints.notes: Meta's official Llama download count. Used in the 'shift to open models' narrative section.

dataPoints.claim: Open models account for 61% of OpenRouter token volume
dataPoints.notes: From OpenRouter's public rankings/statistics. Shows open models dominate by volume even if closed models lead on revenue per query.

dataPoints.claim: Mag 7 AI capex ~$250B+ in 2025
dataPoints.notes: Combined AI-related capital expenditure from Apple, Microsoft, Google, Amazon, Meta, NVIDIA, Tesla. Used in 'What's NOT in this chart' section to show the scale of investment vs actual revenue.

dataPoints.claim: Model training compute spend ~$30-50B in 2025
dataPoints.notes: Estimated total spend on training runs across all major labs. Extremely opaque — no one publishes training costs. Used in 'What's NOT in this chart' section.

dataPoints.claim: Total VC investment into AI ~$150B+ in 2025
dataPoints.notes: Total venture capital deployed into AI companies in 2025. Includes both operating capital and training capex. Used in 'What's NOT in this chart' to show $15:$1 investment-to-revenue ratio.

dataPoints.claim: Token and subscription prices need to rise ~55% to achieve 30% gross margin
dataPoints.notes: At current inference costs ($13B) and customer revenue ($15.6B), achieving 30% GM requires revenue of ~$18.6B. That's a 55% increase on current customer revenue minus SaaS margin. Used in sustainability path #3.

dataPoints.claim: Inference costs need to fall ~35% for minimally viable 30% gross margin
dataPoints.notes: Inference costs need to drop from ~$13B to ~$8.4B for 30% gross margin at current revenue. Used in sustainability path #1.

dataPoints.claim: Shift of ~20-30% of workloads to open models reaches system breakeven
dataPoints.notes: Open models are 5-10x cheaper per token. Moving 20-30% of current closed-model workloads to open models via IaaS providers would bring the overall system to breakeven without hardware cost reduction. Used in sustainability path #2.

dataPoints.claim: AI token prices falling ~80% year-on-year
dataPoints.notes: Observable from public API pricing across all major providers. GPT-4 class models went from ~$30/1M tokens to ~$3-5/1M tokens in 18 months. Used in sustainability path #3 narrative about the tension between needing higher prices and market reality.

dataPoints.claim: Only SaaS + hyperscaler margin exists in the system: $2.15B
dataPoints.notes: Total margin in the Sankey system. Comes from: Trad SaaS vendors $1.75B (Microsoft, Salesforce, ServiceNow keep 62% of their $2.8B) + Hyperscaler cloud pass-through margin $0.4B (20% of $2B). Frontier providers and IaaS providers contribute zero margin.

dataPoints.claim: For every $1 customers spend on AI, investors spend $15+ building it
dataPoints.notes: Customer revenue $15.6B vs total investment $250B+ = roughly 16:1 ratio. Rounded to $15:$1. Used in 'What's NOT in this chart' section.

dataPoints.claim: Anthropic hit $14B ARR in February 2026, grown from $1B in 14 months (12x YoY)
dataPoints.notes: Corroborating — citing widely reported figures. Brad Gerstner (investor) did not dispute.

dataPoints.claim: Anthropic had a $6B revenue month in February 2026 (28-day month)
dataPoints.notes: Authoritative — direct investor with board-level access. Confirms $14B ARR figure.

dataPoints.claim: Anthropic valued at $380B (February 2026)
dataPoints.notes: Corroborating — citing reported valuation.

dataPoints.claim: OpenAI ended 2025 at $20B ARR, grown from $2B to $20B in 24 months
dataPoints.notes: Corroborating — multiple reports. Dashboard updated from $16.8B.

dataPoints.claim: OpenAI valued at $840B (February 2026)
dataPoints.notes: Corroborating — citing reported valuation.

dataPoints.claim: Sarah Friar (Anthropic CFO): every gigawatt = $10B annual revenue
dataPoints.sourceAuthor: Chamath Palihapitiya citing Sarah Friar (Anthropic CFO)
dataPoints.notes: Authoritative — from Anthropic CFO directly. Key metric for DC economics.

dataPoints.claim: Data center all-in cost: $50B per gigawatt (powered shell, land, permits, infrastructure)
dataPoints.sourceAuthor: Chamath Palihapitiya (first-hand, his own DC project in Arizona)
dataPoints.notes: Authoritative — first-hand operator building 1GW DC. Cost went from $10B to $50B.

dataPoints.claim: Data center payback: 5-year break-even, profit in years 6-8
dataPoints.notes: Authoritative — from his own DC economics analysis.

dataPoints.claim: Compute constraint at record high — more constrained than any time in last 3 years
dataPoints.notes: Authoritative — direct investor with visibility into capacity.

dataPoints.claim: NVIDIA invested $40B in OpenAI + Anthropic, expects both to IPO in 2026
dataPoints.notes: Corroborating — Jensen public statement that this is his last private investment.

dataPoints.claim: No proven enterprise ROI from AI at scale — zero examples of sustained positive margin expansion
dataPoints.sourceAuthor: Chamath Palihapitiya (first-hand, runs companies consuming AI)
dataPoints.notes: Authoritative bear case — heavy AI consumer seeing no revenue gains despite tripling token spend.

dataPoints.claim: Token spend tripling every 3 months with no corresponding revenue gains
dataPoints.notes: Authoritative — his own company experience. Key bear case datapoint.

dataPoints.claim: 40% of protested US data centers get canceled — ~7GW at risk (Q1 2026), ~$70B/yr revenue at risk
dataPoints.notes: Indicative — uses Sarah Friar $10B/GW multiplier on canceled capacity.

dataPoints.claim: 85% of startup tokens now on open source models — every startup standing up local models
dataPoints.notes: Indicative — anecdotal from well-connected operator. If true, validates the open model volume story.

dataPoints.claim: Meta spending $115-135B on AI infrastructure in 2026
dataPoints.sourceAuthor: Alex Kantrowitz citing Meta public capex guidance
dataPoints.notes: Authoritative — Meta own public guidance. Midpoint $125B.

dataPoints.claim: OpenAI considering IPO as soon as Q4 2026
dataPoints.sourceAuthor: Alex Kantrowitz citing Wall Street Journal
dataPoints.notes: Corroborating — WSJ source. Aligns with Jensen/Gerstner IPO comments.

dataPoints.claim: Microsoft stock 30% off its high (March 2026)
dataPoints.notes: Corroborating — verifiable market data.

dataPoints.claim: Anthropic annualized revenue $19B (March 2026), up 1,167% YoY
dataPoints.notes: Sacra estimate. NOTE: This is annualized run rate, NOT collected revenue. Our Sankey uses actual collected. Previous Sacra data shows $4.5B for 2025, $14B ARR in Feb 2026. Growth from $875M ARR Jan 2025.

dataPoints.claim: OpenAI annualized revenue ~$25B (March 2026)
dataPoints.notes: Sacra states OpenAI at $25B annualized from $13.1B. NOTE: ARR not collected. Our Sankey had $6B collected for 2025. Need to reconcile — The Information previously reported $13.1B which may be 2025 actual collected.

dataPoints.claim: Anthropic cash flow breakeven timeline delayed beyond 2028 (previously projected 2028)
dataPoints.notes: Anthropic raised 2026 revenue forecast 20% to $18B but simultaneously delayed cash flow positive timeline past 2028. Revenue growth not translating to faster profitability path.

dataPoints.claim: Anthropic Series G: $30B raised at $380B valuation (Feb 2026)
dataPoints.notes: Series G closed Feb 2026. Led by GIC and Coatue. Amazon total investment $8B. Up from $183B valuation in Sep 2025.

dataPoints.claim: OpenAI H1 2025 inference spend $5.0B (exceeds H1 revenue)
dataPoints.notes: OpenAI spent $5.022B on inference in H1 2025 alone — MORE than $4.3B H1 revenue. Full 2024-Sep2025 inference total: $12.4B. This is a critical data point: inference costs EXCEED revenue for OpenAI.

dataPoints.claim: Salesforce Agentforce standalone Q4 FY2026 ARR: $800M (+169% YoY); combined AI + Data 360 FY2026: $2.9B (+200% YoY)
dataPoints.notes: Salesforce Agentforce Q4 FY2026: $800M ARR standalone (+169% YoY). Full FY2026 AI + Data 360 combined: $2.9B (+200% YoY). Also: 19 trillion tokens processed (5x YoY). Official earnings release.

dataPoints.claim: ServiceNow Now Assist ACV $600M+ (Q4 2025), doubled YoY; FY2026 subscription revenue guidance $15.53-15.57B (+20% YoY)
dataPoints.notes: Now Assist ACV $600M+ at Q4 2025, doubled YoY (tracking toward $1B+ for 2026). Total ServiceNow FY2026 sub revenue guided at $15.53-15.57B. AI = ~4% of total sub base — consistent with low-penetration thesis.

dataPoints.claim: OpenRouter processing 1T+ tokens per day as of March 2026; 100T+ tokens/year total throughput
dataPoints.notes: OpenRouter processing 1T+ tokens/day (March 2026). Annual run rate: 100T+ tokens/year. Now routes traffic across 300+ models from 60+ providers, serving 5M+ developers. Key demand-side indicator.

dataPoints.claim: Google Cloud Q4 2025 revenue $17.66B (+47.8% YoY); $240B cloud backlog (doubled YoY) heading into 2026
dataPoints.notes: Google Cloud Q4 2025: $17.66B (+47.8% YoY). Full-year 2025 growth accelerated through H2. $240B backlog doubled YoY — indicates committed future AI cloud revenue. Vertex AI cited as key driver.

dataPoints.claim: Anthropic Claude Opus 4.6 API repriced to $5/$25 per M input/output tokens; long-context (1M token) surcharge eliminated for Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6
dataPoints.notes: Opus 4.6: $5/$25 per M in/out tokens (was higher; ~67% cut). Sonnet 4.6: $3/$15. Haiku 4.5: $1/$5. Long-context surcharge removed — 1M context window now at standard per-token rates.

dataPoints.claim: Chamath states he is personally paying AI models millions of dollars a year in token costs, consuming more tokens every single day, but not seeing proportional revenue growth from his business.
Chamath says his AI token spending is tripling every 3 months but his revenues are not tripling correspondingly.

Speaker is building a 1-gigawatt data center in Arizona that started as a $4-5 billion investment estimate but has grown to upwards of $50 billion when including powered shell, land, permits, infrastructure, and people.
Speaker states that open source models are picking up about 85% of their tokens right now, with paid frontier models only used for jobs that can't be done otherwise.
Anthropic 2025 actual revenue confirmed ~$4.5B (Sacra historical)
dataPoints.notes: Sacra confirms our existing $4.5B figure for Anthropic 2025 revenue. Cross-referenced with The Information reporting. Validates current Sankey number.

dataPoints.claim: Claude Code annualized revenue >$2.5B (Feb 2026)
dataPoints.notes: Claude Code hit $2.5B ARR in Feb 2026, doubled since start of 2026 (was $500M ARR in Sep 2025). Major new revenue stream — not broken out in our current Sankey.

dataPoints.claim: Anthropic: 70-75% revenue from API, 10-15% from subscriptions
dataPoints.notes: Revenue breakdown: 70-75% token-based API, 10-15% subscriptions, remainder reserved capacity/enterprise. Useful for Sankey channel allocation (Model Subs vs API vs Enterprise).

dataPoints.claim: Anthropic has 300,000+ business customers, 500 spending >$1M/yr
dataPoints.notes: As of Oct 2025: 300K+ biz customers (~80% of revenue), 500 spending >$1M/yr (up from 12 two years ago). 8 of Fortune 10 are Claude customers.

dataPoints.claim: Claude Code: 29M daily VS Code installs, 4% of public GitHub commits
dataPoints.notes: Claude Code developer adoption metrics. 29M daily VS Code installs. 4% of all public GitHub commits.

dataPoints.claim: Gemini: 650M monthly users vs ChatGPT 800M weekly users
dataPoints.notes: User comparison: Gemini 650M monthly vs ChatGPT 800M weekly (note different measurement periods). Google boosted by Nano Banana launch.

dataPoints.claim: OpenAI H1 2025 actual collected revenue ~$4.3B
dataPoints.notes: The Information reported $4.3B H1 2025 revenue. Zitron's MSFT rev share analysis implies it may be closer to $2.27B — a ~$2B gap. Used $4.3B as the more conservative (higher) figure.

dataPoints.claim: OpenAI $25B ARR uses best 4-week period × 12 (not monthly × 12)
dataPoints.notes: CRITICAL: OpenAI's $25B ARR confirmed to use best 4-week period × 12, NOT calendar month × 12. This inflates the figure by an estimated 10-15% vs monthly calculation. Must apply cherry-pick haircut in ARR conversion.

dataPoints.claim: OpenAI funding round extended to $120B total at $840B post-money valuation (March 2026)
dataPoints.notes: OpenAI added extra $10B to existing round, bringing total to $120B at $840B post-money valuation. Significantly higher than Anthropic's $380B valuation.

dataPoints.claim: OpenAI projected 2026 cash burn ~$25B
dataPoints.notes: OpenAI projected to burn ~$25B in 2026 even as revenue hits $25B ARR. Implies close to breakeven on ARR basis but not on actual collected revenue. Critical context for burn rate analysis.

dataPoints.claim: OpenAI internal 2030 revenue forecast: $280B
dataPoints.notes: OpenAI's internal 2030 revenue forecast is $280B. Context: current 2025 actual ~$13B, implies 21x growth in 5 years. Highly speculative — flagged for perspective/context use only.

dataPoints.claim: Microsoft AI business $13B ARR (Q2 FY2026, Jan 2026); Copilot 15M paid seats, 3.3% of M365 commercial base
dataPoints.notes: Microsoft AI at $13B ARR Q2 FY2026. Copilot: 15M paid seats (+160% YoY) = 3.3% of 450M commercial M365 base. Key enterprise adoption data point — 2+ years in, still only 1-in-30 users paying.

dataPoints.claim: Harvey (legal AI) raised $200M at $11B valuation (March 25, 2026)
dataPoints.notes: Harvey raised $200M at $11B valuation. Legal AI vertical — notable as sector-specific AI company reaching unicorn+ status.

dataPoints.claim: Physical Intelligence (ex-DeepMind robotics) in talks for ~$1B round at $11B+ valuation (March 27, 2026)
dataPoints.notes: Physical Intelligence (robotics startup, ex-DeepMind staff) in talks for ~$1B at $11B+ valuation. AI investment extending into physical AI/robotics sector.

dataPoints.claim: Anthropic 2026 internal revenue forecast raised 20% to $18B
dataPoints.notes: Anthropic raised its internal 2026 revenue forecast by 20% to $18B. Note: this is a forecast for actual collected revenue (not ARR), distinct from the $19B current ARR run rate.

dataPoints.claim: February 2026 was largest startup funding month ever: $189B globally; AI startups = 41% of all VC dollars
dataPoints.notes: Feb 2026 record driven by OpenAI $110B + Anthropic $30B + Waymo $16B. AI = 41% of all venture dollars on Carta. Highly concentrated — 3 deals = ~83% of the record month.

dataPoints.claim: OpenAI projected operating loss $14B in 2026 (tripling from ~$5B in 2025)
dataPoints.notes: OpenAI internal projections imply $14B operating loss in 2026 — tripling from ~$5B in 2025. Burn rate ~57% of projected revenue. Critical context: losses growing faster than revenue.

dataPoints.claim: OpenAI cumulative cash burn $115B through 2029
dataPoints.notes: OpenAI says its business will burn $115B cumulatively through 2029. Projects profitability at $100B revenue in 2029. Contrasts with Anthropic target of 9% burn rate by 2027.

dataPoints.claim: OpenAI burn rate 57% of revenue in 2026-2027; Anthropic burn dropping to 9% of revenue by 2027
dataPoints.notes: OpenAI burn 57% of revenue through 2027. Anthropic projects burn dropping to ~33% in 2026 and 9% by 2027 — structural divergence in economics between the two leading frontier labs.

dataPoints.claim: Anthropic gross margin projection lowered ~10 percentage points vs prior forecast due to rising inference costs
dataPoints.notes: Anthropic cut gross margin projection by ~10pp as cloud server/inference costs outpaced revenue growth. Both OpenAI and Anthropic missed their own gross margin forecasts. Signals compute cost pressure despite rapid revenue growth.

dataPoints.claim: OpenRouter March 2026: MiniMax M2.5 #1 by token volume (4.55T tokens), Moonshot Kimi K2.5 #2 (4.02T tokens); Chinese models ~2/3 of top-5 token consumption
dataPoints.notes: Chinese models dominate OpenRouter by token volume in March 2026. MiniMax M2.5: 4.55T tokens (#1), Kimi K2.5: 4.02T (#2), DeepSeek #4. Chinese models = ~2/3 of top-5 consumption. Top-5 includes only one Anthropic model.

dataPoints.claim: H100 cloud GPU spot pricing fell to ~$2.99/hr in Q1 2026, down 64-75% from $8-10/hr in Q4 2024
dataPoints.notes: H100 spot pricing ~$2.99/hr (Q1 2026) vs $8-10/hr (Q4 2024). Specialized GPU providers 60-85% cheaper than hyperscalers at $0.29-$2.99/hr.

dataPoints.claim: LLM inference cost collapsed ~1,000x: GPT-4-class model $20/M tokens (late 2022) → ~$0.40/M tokens (early 2026)
dataPoints.notes: From $20/M tokens (Nov 2022) to $0.40/M tokens (early 2026) for GPT-4 class performance — a ~1,000x reduction in 3 years. Driven by hardware advances, competition, efficiency gains.

dataPoints.claim: Inference accounts for ~2/3 of all AI compute workloads in 2026, overtaking training as the dominant workload
dataPoints.notes: Inference now ~2/3 of all AI compute (up from minority share in 2023). Training has been overtaken as the dominant AI workload — structurally important for GPU demand forecasting.

dataPoints.claim: Qwen (Alibaba) 400M+ Hugging Face downloads; Chinese open models overtook US models in total hub downloads
dataPoints.notes: Qwen: 400M+ downloads; OpenAI/DeepSeek ~50M each. 113K+ Qwen derivative models. China = 41% of Hugging Face downloads. Chinese open models now dominant on largest ML hub.

dataPoints.claim: Nscale (AI compute infrastructure) raised $2B Series C at $14.6B valuation (Q1 2026)
dataPoints.notes: Nscale $2B Series C at $14.6B valuation. AI compute infra now a sovereign-level investment priority. Signals large-scale private capital flowing into GPU/inference infrastructure beyond the frontier labs.

dataPoints.claim: A McKinsey study showed that a lot of pilot projects in Fortune 500 companies were experimental and a lot of them were proving not to be successful.
About 80% of people in China said AI would be more beneficial than harmful. In the US, it was in the 30s.
Sam Altman stated that fundamentally OpenAI's business, and the business of every other model provider, is going to look like selling tokens.

OpenAI is discontinuing Sora consumer app, the developer API, and video functionality inside ChatGPT as part of a refocus on business and coding functions ahead of a potential IPO as soon as Q4 this year.
OpenAI spending ratio: $2.25-$2.60 to make $1 in revenue
dataPoints.notes: OpenAI spending $2.25-$2.60 for every $1 earned. Implies ~60% of total spend is just inference, rest is R&D ($6.7B H1), sales/marketing ($2B H1), and other ops.

dataPoints.claim: Anthropic hit a $14 billion run rate last month, February. That means they have grown revenue from $1 billion to $14 billion in 14 months — 12x year over year.
Anthropic grew revenue from $1 billion to $14 billion in 14 months, representing approximately 12x year-over-year growth.
Anthropic was valued at $380 billion last month.
OpenAI ended 2025 at a $20 billion annualized run rate, growing revenue from $2 billion to $20 billion in 24 months.
OpenAI was valued at $840 billion last month.
Jensen Huang said he expected the $40 billion he recently invested in OpenAI and Anthropic would be his last money in because they were both going to go public this year.
Consumer subscriptions are roughly half of OpenAI's revenue; consumer subscribers at ~$20/month are clearly getting value.
Sarah Friar said that for every gigawatt, it's about $10 billion of annual revenue.
For every gigawatt of spend, there is roughly a 5-year payback period just to get to break even, with profit coming in years 6, 7, and 8.
It's about $50 billion per gigawatt of data center cost and about a 5 to 6 year payback just to get into the money, then about $10 billion a year in revenue.
In 2025, about 25 data centers were canceled, representing about 5 gigawatts. Using Sarah Friar's number, that's $50 billion a year of revenue off the table.
As of end of February 2026, about 100 data centers are being protested, representing about 7 gigawatts, which at $10B/GW equals $70 billion a year of revenue at risk.
In 2025 and 2026 combined, approximately $120 billion of cumulative annual revenue has been taken off the table due to data center cancellations.
About 40% of all protested data centers in America get canceled.
Meta is spending 115 to 135 billion on AI infrastructure this year.
OpenAI has 800 million users and is aiming to get to a billion.
OpenAI closed its latest funding round with $122 billion in committed capital
dataPoints.notes: Auto-extracted from Open AI Press release 31 Mar

dataPoints.notes: Auto-extracted from Open AI Press release 31 Mar

dataPoints.notes: Auto-extracted from Open AI Press release 31 Mar

dataPoints.claim: OpenRouter model rankings by token volume: Anthropic #1 (1.1T tokens, -1.6%/wk), OpenAI #2 (382B tokens, +19.3%/wk), Google #3 (323B tokens, +1.9%/wk)
dataPoints.notes: OpenRouter live rankings snapshot Apr 2026. Anthropic leads with 1.1T total but declining -1.6%/wk. OpenAI surging +19.3%/wk (382B tokens). OSS models ~1/3 of usage; Chinese OSS near 30% some weeks.

OpenAI has a post money valuation of $852 billion

dataPoints.claim: Ramp data suggested that Anthropic now captures 73% of all new spending among companies buying AI tools; 10 weeks ago it was 50/50 with OpenAI; early December it was 60/40 in OpenAI's favor.
dataPoints.notes: Podcast: 20VC — 20VC: Why You Need a $1BN Fund To Do Series A Today | OpenAI

dataPoints.claim: 10 weeks ago Anthropic and OpenAI were 50/50 in new AI tool spending share according to Ramp data.

dataPoints.claim: Early December, OpenAI had 60% vs Anthropic's 40% of new AI tool spending according to Ramp data.

dataPoints.claim: OpenAI is still ahead of Anthropic in terms of total spend, but the marginal buyer in the last 6-10 weeks has massively shifted, with 70% going to Anthropic.
dataPoints.notes: Podcast: 20VC — 20VC: Why You Need a $1BN Fund To Do Series A Today | OpenAI

dataPoints.claim: Groq was acquired by Nvidia for $20 billion; Groq had less than $100 million in ARR at the time of the deal.
dataPoints.notes: Podcast: 20VC — 20VC: Why You Need a $1BN Fund To Do Series A Today | OpenAI

dataPoints.claim: Groq had approximately $100 million in revenue at the time of the $20 billion Nvidia deal.
dataPoints.notes: Podcast: 20VC — 20VC: Why You Need a $1BN Fund To Do Series A Today | OpenAI

dataPoints.claim: Groq's last funding round valued the company at $6.9 billion before the $20 billion Nvidia acquisition.
dataPoints.notes: Podcast: 20VC — 20VC: Why You Need a $1BN Fund To Do Series A Today | OpenAI

dataPoints.claim: Crusoe has over a million GPUs in their fleet and they're valued at over $10 billion.
dataPoints.notes: Podcast: Acquired — The NFL

dataPoints.claim: Crusoe has an astonishing total of 45 gigawatts in their pipeline.

dataPoints.claim: NVIDIA spent approximately $20 billion acquiring Groq because they lacked a fast inference answer; fast inference requires fast memory access which GPUs don't have.

dataPoints.notes: Podcast: All-In Podcast — Transcript of The Future of Everything: What CEOs of Circle,

dataPoints.claim: Anthropic drawing investor offers at $800B+ valuation (April 2026), up from $350B pre-money valuation at February Series G
dataPoints.notes: Bloomberg Apr 14: Anthropic received VC/investor offers at $800B+ valuation but hasn't accepted. Would be >2x the $350B pre-money valuation from Feb 2026 Series G. Driven by $30B ARR milestone. Offers resisted as of publication.

dataPoints.claim: Xiaomi MiMo V2 Pro #1 on OpenRouter in April 2026 with 4.65T tokens/week; Xiaomi commands 22.3% of OpenRouter market share
dataPoints.notes: MiMo-V2-Pro (Xiaomi) tops OpenRouter April 2026 with 4.65T tokens/week — >2x Claude Sonnet 4.6 volume. Xiaomi at 22.3% platform share. Was deployed stealth as 'Hunter Alpha' before identity revealed March 18.

dataPoints.claim: Anthropic IPO target as early as October 2026; Bloomberg reports unsolicited investor offers at $800B+ valuation
dataPoints.notes: Multiple reports indicate Anthropic evaluating IPO as early as Oct 2026. Bloomberg: unsolicited offers at $800B+ (>2x Series G $380B post-money).

dataPoints.claim: OpenAI confirmed $2B/month revenue (~$24B ARR) at $122B funding close (March 31, 2026)
dataPoints.notes: OpenAI stated $2B/month revenue (~$24B ARR) at time of $122B funding announcement. Full-year 2025 revenue cited as $13.1B.

dataPoints.claim: Microsoft launched M365 E7 enterprise plan at $99/user/month including Copilot (March 2026), 65% above prior top tier
dataPoints.notes: Microsoft M365 E7 at $99/user/month with Copilot bundled. 65% price premium vs prior top tier. Framed as offsetting $72B capex over prior 2 quarters.
